name: qBittorrent
description: Lightweight torrent client with a clean web UI and automation-friendly API.

extra_vars.description: Web UI port (must match host port)

steps.instruction: Login with username = admin and the temporary password shown in the wizard box below. qBittorrent 4.6+ generates a random password on first start — the old admin/adminadmin default no longer works
Go to Tools (top menu) → Options → Web UI tab. Under 'Authentication', change the Password field to a strong password. Click Save at the bottom

HomeStack tries to set 'Default Save Path' to /data/downloads automatically. Open Options → Downloads and verify it shows /data/downloads. If it still shows /downloads, change it manually and click Save

HomeStack also tries to allow the internal HomeStack Docker network to reach qBittorrent without storing your new password in the *arr apps. If you later tighten Web UI authentication settings, revisit the Download Clients section in Radarr/Sonarr/Whisparr
